title: rumex aquaticus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Rumex aquaticus subsp. occidentalis, also known as western dock or water dock, is a subspecies of the Rumex aquaticus plant, a perennial herb native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Rumex aquaticus subsp. occidentalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. Rumex aquaticus subsp. occidentalis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
